entities.name: brahman
entities.cite: The opening karika states the sabdadvaita thesis in a single breath

entities.name: śabdatattva
entities.cite: the ultimate is not merely describable by language but is language — sabda-tattva, word-essence

entities.name: jagat
entities.cite: The world issues from Brahman the way a word-form issues from its base

axioms.cite: the ultimate is not merely describable by language but is language — sabda-tattva, word-essence

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: Vivarta is appearance without loss of unity: the one presents itself as many while remaining one

axioms.name: anadinidhana
axioms.cite: its plain sense is 'imperishable', which is why it sits naturally beside anadinidhana, 'without beginning or end'

axioms.name: aksara
axioms.cite: What is imperishable and what is phonic are, for him, the same thing

denials.name: parinamate
denials.cite: Bhartrhari does not say Brahman becomes the world (parinamate), which would make the world a real transformation of its substance

claims.name: vivartate
claims.cite: It appears in the mode of objects

rejected_readings.label: naive_linguistic_structure
rejected_readings.rendering: The imperishable linguistic structure underlying words has no beginning or end.
rejected_readings.why: Renders śabdatattva as a mere linguistic property, demoting the ontological identity the commentary insists on: the ultimate IS language, not something described by it. The sort error (property vs absolute) makes the identity claim decidably false.

rejected_readings.label: parinama_transformation
rejected_readings.rendering: Brahman transforms itself into the world of objects.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts a real transformation (parinama) of Brahman's substance, which the commentary explicitly rules out in favor of vivarta (appearance without loss of unity).

notes: Contested: whether vivarta already carries the later Advaitic sense of illusory appearance is disputed; the contract deliberately encodes only the weaker commitment (manifestation) shared by both readings.
